ab: inta zazuwa kemureta ta nu ’emanan nu alusu kadaw mu?
ch: W:我和您約明天下午好嗎?
en: Can I make an appointment with you tomorrow afternoon?

ab: ai, uri paretratrevung ta i iyan?
ch: M:可以，我們約哪裡見面?
en: Sure, where should we meet?

ab: maretrevunga ta i kungkuwan mu? kisaasing ta kana iga.
ch: W:我們約學校好嗎?我們要錄影。
en: How about meeting at school? We need to shoot a video.

ab: nu kemazu mu kay ta i palakuwan, piyakavang ku kana ta kavang na kinawatranan mukuwa.
ch: M:那去聚會所好了，我會穿族服去。
en: Then let’s go to the community center. I’ll wear folk costume.

ab: nu mi’aputr na sakura kaniyam zekalr mu salaw vulay. ngarayu nu mi’aputr nu azu mu alamu lra aya’u ku!
ch: W: 我們部落的櫻花盛開很漂亮。等花開的時候你來找我吧!
en: The cherry blossoms in our tribe are beautiful when they bloom. Come find me when the flowers bloom!

ab: nu kemazu mu ngangara ta nu kaseveran lra, piwari lra zazu.
ch: M: 那要等到春天，太晚了吧!
en: That would have to wait until spring. It’s too late, isn’t it?

ab: nu kemazu mu alamu lra nu kavarasa’an!
ch: W: 那你豐年祭的時候來吧!
en: Then come during our Harvest Festival!

ab: ’inava wa! salaw ku mengangara!
ch: M: 好啊!我很期待!
en: Alright! I’m looking forward to it!

ab: kiumalra ku kaninu mazu zi mupasalr u zuwa i zekalr kazuwa?
ch: W: 請問你為甚麼要搬回部落生活?
en: May I ask why you decided to move back to the tribe?

ab: waruma’ ku awa ku patakesiya.
ch: M: 我要回去教書。
en: I want to return to teach at the local school.

ab: mazu ’azi u patakesi i tokay?
ch: W: 為甚麼不在都市教書?
en: Why not teach in the city?

ab: kana sare’az ku kana ku zekalr, zi pavulaliyas ku i zekalr zaku ikasaayhu.
ch: M: 因為我懷念我的故鄉，也希望能回饋部落。
en: Because I miss my hometown and I also want to give back to the tribe.

ab: sayhu u semilretrav, maw i eman na panadam kaninu?
ch: M:你很會織布，是誰教你的?
en: You’re very skilled at weaving, who taught you?

ab: maw i mulri na vavayan, i intaw mu maw na marasayhuyhu semilretrav kaniyam zekalr na trau.
ch: W:是我的奶奶，她是我們部落最會織布的人。
en: It was my grandmother, she is the most skilled weaver in our tribe.

ab: mazu mianger u za silralretravan?
ch: M:你怎麼會想要學織布?
en: How did you develop an interest in learning weaving?

ab: na semilralretrav mu niniyam kakuwayanan na kinawatranan, maranger ku tremungutrungulr kananiyam kakuwayanan na kinawatranan.
ch: W:織布是我們的文化，我想要讓文化延續。
en: Weaving is a part of our culture, and I want to ensure its continuity.

ab: ini na tinuwakutra kana dawa mu tatena’ imaran.
ch: M:這個小米酒真好喝。
en: This millet wine is delicious.

ab: ninku tinuwakutra yi!
ch: W:是我自己釀的喔!
en: I made it myself!

ab: tatena’ u kana sayhu. nu tremuwakutra ta mu sawariyan?
ch: M:你真厲害，這要釀很久嗎?
en: You’re really talented. Does it take a long time to make?

ab: ’azi, aruwa saliyusan maruwa. ta puwanay i lidingan demuru na lriketran nu maderu na lriketran nu lritek lra mu ta puwayay kana pikak ta triwtriwraw, zi ta puwanay lra i urauratran.
ch: W:不用，大約一周就好了。把糯米蒸熟，再加入麴粉一起拌勻，就可以裝到甕裡了。
en: No, it only takes about a week. After steaming the sticky rice and white rice, we mix it with yeast powder, and then it can be stored in a jar.
